proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients carrying npm1_mutation have a higher rate of objective_response than patients without npm1_mutation.
Patients carrying tp53_mutation have a lower rate of objective_response than patients without tp53_mutation.
Patients with complex_karyotype have a lower rate of objective_response than patients without complex_karyotype.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate is 0.235 in NPM1+ vs 0.167 in NPM1- (n=15014 vs 34986).
Response rate is 0.166 in TP53+ vs 0.190 in TP53- (n=4985 vs 45015).
Response rate is 0.168 in complex_karyotype+ vs 0.191 in complex_karyotype- (n=7462 vs 42538).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients flagged unfit_for_intensive have a higher rate of objective_response than fit patients (because the unfit subgroup is preferentially treated with venetoclax-based therapy).
Patients with secondary_aml have a different rate of objective_response than de novo patients.
Female sex is associated with a different rate of objective_response than male sex.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate is 0.221 in unfit vs 0.165 in fit (n=20056 vs 29944).
Response rate is 0.188 in secondary_aml vs 0.187 in de novo; no detectable difference.
Response rate is 0.189 in females vs 0.186 in males; no detectable difference.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher blast_pct_marrow is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Mean ecog_ps is 0.669 in responders vs 0.826 in non-responders (lower ECOG -> higher response).
Mean blast_pct_marrow is 58.50 in responders vs 60.30 in non-responders.
Mean weight_loss_pct_6mo is 3.44 in responders vs 3.95 in non-responders.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.
Higher wbc_k_per_ul is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Mean albumin is 3.824 in responders vs 3.795 in non-responders.
Mean WBC is 19.27 in responders vs 20.39 in non-responders.
Mean CRP is 5.69 in responders vs 6.17 in non-responders.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FLT3-ITD is associated with a different rate of objective_response than FLT3-ITD-negative status.
IDH1 mutation is associated with a different rate of objective_response than IDH1-wild-type.
IDH2 mutation is associated with a different rate of objective_response than IDH2-wild-type.
Routine chemistries (sodium, potassium, calcium, creatinine, BUN, AST, ALT, total bilirubin) show no clinically meaningful association with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 0.189 in FLT3-ITD+ vs 0.187 in FLT3-ITD-; no detectable difference at the population level.
Response rate 0.191 in IDH1+ vs 0.187 in IDH1-; no detectable difference.
Response rate 0.191 in IDH2+ vs 0.187 in IDH2-; no detectable difference.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on each chemistry by response
analyses.result_summary: AST p=0.128, ALT p=0.195, total bilirubin p=0.216, creatinine p=0.716, BUN p=0.548, sodium p=0.616, potassium p=0.764, calcium p=0.813; alkaline phosphatase borderline (p=0.0511) with mean +1.1 U/L in responders. None of these chemistries are clinically meaningful.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with higher objective_response than no treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
treatment_7plus3 is associated with higher objective_response than no treatment_7plus3.
treatment_midostaurin is associated with a different rate of objective_response than no treatment_midostaurin.
treatment_gilteritinib is associated with a different rate of objective_response than no treatment_gilteritinib.
treatment_ivosidenib is associated with a different rate of objective_response than no treatment_ivosidenib.
treatment_enasidenib is associated with a different rate of objective_response than no treatment_enasidenib.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 0.220 on ven_aza vs 0.166 off (n=20020 vs 29980).
Response rate 0.189 on 7+3 vs 0.186 off (n=22580 vs 27420).
Response rate 0.186 on midostaurin vs 0.188 off (n=7545 vs 42455).
Response rate 0.181 on gilteritinib vs 0.189 off (n=7545 vs 42455).
Response rate 0.188 on ivosidenib vs 0.187 off (n=3938 vs 46062).
Response rate 0.186 on enasidenib vs 0.188 off (n=3972 vs 46028).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment assignment is independent of biomarker status (i.e., treatments were assigned approximately at random with respect to NPM1, TP53, FLT3, IDH1, IDH2, complex karyotype, secondary AML, and unfit_for_intensive).

analyses.code: for each (tx, biomarker): compare P(tx=1 | bm=1) to P(tx=1 | bm=0)
analyses.result_summary: Across all 6 treatments and 9 biomarkers (54 cells), the absolute differences in treatment prevalence between biomarker-positive and biomarker-negative patients are all <=1.4 percentage points (largest seen: ven_aza +1.2 pp in TP53+ and -1.4 pp in FLT3-TKD+; ivosidenib -1.1 pp in FLT3-TKD+). This is consistent with effectively random treatment assignment, so subsequent treatment effects do not require confounding adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x npm1_mutation interaction on objective_response: ven_aza increases response substantially more in NPM1+ than in NPM1- patients.

analyses.result_summary: Within NPM1+ (n=15014): rr_on=0.347, rr_off=0.160 (diff=+0.187, p=1.24e-154). Within NPM1- (n=34986): rr_on=0.165, rr_off=0.169 (diff=-0.004, p=0.336). Logistic interaction term coef=+1.055 (log-odds), p=5.5e-102.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x unfit_for_intensive interaction on objective_response: ven_aza increases response substantially more in unfit-for-intensive than in fit patients.

analyses.result_summary: Within unfit (n=20056): rr_on=0.308, rr_off=0.163 (diff=+0.145, p=4.89e-130). Within fit (n=29944): rr_on=0.160, rr_off=0.168 (diff=-0.008, p=0.080). Logistic interaction coef=+0.883, p=1.14e-78.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a negative treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x tp53_mutation interaction on objective_response: ven_aza fails to improve response in TP53+ patients while it does improve response in TP53- patients.
There is a negative treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x complex_karyotype interaction on objective_response: ven_aza's benefit is markedly attenuated in complex_karyotype patients.

analyses.result_summary: Within TP53+ (n=4985): rr_on=0.164, rr_off=0.167 (diff=-0.004, p=0.736). Within TP53- (n=45015): rr_on=0.226, rr_off=0.166 (diff=+0.060, p=6.75e-57). Logistic interaction coef=-0.409, p=4.67e-07.
Within CK+ (n=7462): rr_on=0.175, rr_off=0.163 (diff=+0.012, p=0.192). Within CK- (n=42538): rr_on=0.227, rr_off=0.167 (diff=+0.061, p=2.66e-55). Logistic interaction coef=-0.306, p=6.0e-06.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x flt3_itd interaction on objective_response is null (no differential benefit by FLT3-ITD status).
treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x idh1_mutation interaction on objective_response is null.
treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x idh2_mutation interaction on objective_response is null.
treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine x secondary_aml interaction on objective_response is null.

analyses.result_summary: Within FLT3-ITD+ diff=+0.060 (p=8.95e-14); within FLT3-ITD- diff=+0.052 (p=5.47e-39). Interaction coef=+0.045, p=0.429: not significant.
Within IDH1+ diff=+0.050; within IDH1- diff=+0.054. Interaction coef=-0.032, p=0.718.
Within IDH2+ diff=+0.056; within IDH2- diff=+0.053. Interaction coef=+0.011, p=0.892.
Within secondary_aml diff=+0.055; within de novo diff=+0.053. Interaction coef=+0.013, p=0.804.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Joint logistic model: ven_aza interactions with npm1_mutation (positive), unfit_for_intensive (positive), tp53_mutation (negative), and complex_karyotype (negative) are simultaneously significant after mutual adjustment, indicating four independent modifiers.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine * npm1_mutation + treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine * unfit_for_intensive + treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine * tp53_mutation + treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine * complex_karyotype', df)
analyses.result_summary: All four interactions remain significant after mutual adjustment: ven_aza:npm1 +1.104 (p=2.6e-108), ven_aza:unfit +0.935 (p=2.5e-89), ven_aza:tp53 -0.475 (p=1.1e-08), ven_aza:CK -0.340 (p=8.4e-07). The four modifiers act independently on a multiplicative scale.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_7plus3 has no benefit on objective_response in any biomarker subgroup (no main effect, no positive interaction).
treatment_7plus3 may be harmful in complex_karyotype patients (negative subgroup effect).

analyses.code: subgroup screen of treatment_7plus3 across all biomarkers
analyses.result_summary: treatment_7plus3 effect by subgroup: fit diff=+0.004 (p=0.37); unfit diff=0.000 (p=0.98); FLT3+ diff=+0.006 (p=0.37); FLT3- diff=+0.001 (p=0.74); NPM1+ diff=-0.006 (p=0.38); NPM1- diff=+0.006 (p=0.16); TP53+ diff=-0.003 (p=0.75); TP53- diff=+0.003 (p=0.39); secondary diff=-0.006 (p=0.37); de novo diff=+0.006 (p=0.17). No subgroup with positive treatment effect.

Within complex_karyotype+ (n=7462): rr_on=0.155, rr_off=0.179 (diff=-0.023, p=0.0077). Mildly negative effect, but not adjusted for multiple comparisons; consistent with intensive chemotherapy underperforming in CK+ disease.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_midostaurin improves objective_response in FLT3-mutant patients (FLT3-ITD or FLT3-TKD).
treatment_gilteritinib improves objective_response in FLT3-mutant patients.

analyses.result_summary: Within FLT3+ (any) (n=12815): rr_on=0.176, rr_off=0.189 (diff=-0.013, p=0.184). No benefit detected; if anything a non-significant trend toward lower response. Even within fit FLT3+ TP53- CK- (n=5879): diff=-0.017 (p=0.21).
Within FLT3+ (any) (n=12815): rr_on=0.182, rr_off=0.188 (diff=-0.006, p=0.55). No benefit detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_ivosidenib improves objective_response in IDH1-mutant patients.
treatment_enasidenib improves objective_response in IDH2-mutant patients.

analyses.result_summary: Within IDH1+ (n=3624): rr_on=0.154, rr_off=0.194 (diff=-0.040, p=0.094). No detectable benefit, in fact trend toward lower response.
Within IDH2+ (n=4679): rr_on=0.195, rr_off=0.191 (diff=+0.004, p=0.865). No detectable benefit overall; within unfit IDH2+ a non-significant +0.060 (p=0.108) but n=141 limits power.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher blast_pct_marrow positively modifies the ven_aza treatment effect (treatment effect grows with blast burden).
ECOG performance status does not modify the ven_aza treatment effect on the absolute scale.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo positively modifies the ven_aza treatment effect.

analyses.result_summary: Logistic interaction coef=+0.0035 per blast %, p=0.0035. Subgroup confirmation: ven_aza diff in blast tertiles: low=+0.046 (p=8e-13), mid=+0.049 (p=3e-15), high=+0.066 (p=6e-29). Modest but real positive modification.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine * ecog_ps', df); subgroup ven_aza diff by ECOG
analyses.result_summary: Logistic interaction p=0.279. Absolute ven_aza differences: ECOG=0 +0.060 (p=4e-20), ECOG=1 +0.051 (p=1e-25), ECOG=2 +0.049 (p=6e-10). Effect is comparable across ECOG levels on the absolute scale.
Interaction coef=+0.0129 per pct, p=0.039. Borderline positive modification on log-odds scale.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adjusted for treatments and other features in a single multivariable logistic model, ven_aza retains a strong positive effect on objective_response while none of the other five treatments shows an independent effect.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ all 6 treatments + age + sex_female + ecog_ps + secondary_aml + unfit_for_intensive + complex_karyotype + flt3_itd + flt3_tkd + idh1 + idh2 + npm1 + tp53 + wbc + blast + albumin + weight_loss + crp', df)
analyses.result_summary: ven_aza beta=+0.361, p<1e-50; midostaurin beta=-0.010 p=0.755; gilteritinib beta=-0.049 p=0.133; ivosidenib beta=+0.019 p=0.660; enasidenib beta=-0.010 p=0.813; 7+3 beta=+0.015 p=0.526. Among covariates: NPM1+ +0.436 (p<1e-50), unfit +0.373 (p<1e-50), ECOG -0.361 (p<1e-50), albumin +0.123 (p=2e-7), weight_loss -0.037 (p<1e-30), CK -0.169 (p=8e-7), TP53 -0.188 (p=4e-6), blast_pct -0.005 (p<1e-16), CRP -0.006 (p=7e-6), WBC -0.002 (p=0.0001). Confirms ven_aza as the only effective treatment in this cohort and locks in the prognostic feature set.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In NPM1+ AND unfit_for_intensive patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine produces an extreme positive treatment effect on objective_response (>40 percentage points absolute).
Within the NPM1+ AND unfit subgroup, restricting further to TP53- AND complex_karyotype- yields an even larger positive ven_aza effect (>=60 percentage points).

analyses.result_summary: n_on=2401, n_off=3585. rr_on=0.633, rr_off=0.160 (diff=+0.474, p=2.36e-310). Treatment effect is roughly 9x the population-average effect.
n_on=1790, n_off=2741. rr_on=0.786, rr_off=0.158 (diff=+0.628, p<1e-300). Adding TP53- and CK- to the NPM1+/unfit definition raises the absolute treatment effect to about 63 percentage points.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the inverse subgroup (TP53+ OR complex_karyotype+), ven_aza has no detectable benefit on objective_response.
In TP53+ patients alone, ven_aza has no detectable benefit.
In NPM1- AND fit (i.e., neither favorable modifier present) patients, ven_aza has no benefit (or a slight negative trend).

analyses.result_summary: n_on=4737, n_off=6942. rr_on=0.171, rr_off=0.165 (diff=+0.006, p=0.357). No detectable benefit.
n_on=2048, n_off=2937. rr_on=0.164, rr_off=0.167 (diff=-0.004, p=0.736). No benefit in TP53-mutant disease.
n_on=8383, n_off=12533. rr_on=0.162, rr_off=0.172 (diff=-0.010, p=0.067). Slight negative trend in patients with neither favorable modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within NPM1+ patients, complex_karyotype suppresses the ven_aza effect (i.e., the NPM1+ benefit holds only when CK is absent).
Within NPM1+ patients, tp53_mutation suppresses the ven_aza effect.
Within NPM1+ AND TP53- AND CK- patients, ven_aza produces a large response benefit even without the unfit_for_intensive flag (so unfit augments but does not gate the effect).

analyses.code: proportions_ztest on ven_aza within (npm1==1) & (CK==1) and within (npm1==1) & (CK==0)
analyses.result_summary: Within NPM1+ AND CK- (n=12772): rr_on=0.367, rr_off=0.158 (diff=+0.208, p<1e-150). Within NPM1+ AND CK+ (n=2242): rr_on=0.225, rr_off=0.171 (diff=+0.054, p=0.018). Effect ~4x larger when CK is absent.

Within NPM1+ AND TP53- (n=13508): rr_on=0.367, rr_off=0.159 (diff=+0.208, p=2.93e-167). Within NPM1+ AND TP53+ (n=1506): rr_on=0.180, rr_off=0.167 (diff=+0.014, p=0.49). TP53 mutation almost completely abolishes the NPM1+ ven_aza benefit.
n_on=4579, n_off=6903. rr_on=0.403, rr_off=0.160 (diff=+0.243, p=2.81e-187). Even without the unfit flag, NPM1+/TP53-/CK- patients gain ~24 percentage points from ven_aza.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within unfit_for_intensive patients, the absence of TP53 and complex_karyotype mutations is required for full ven_aza benefit.

analyses.code: proportions_ztest on ven_aza within unfit & TP53==0 and within unfit & (TP53==1 | CK==1)
analyses.result_summary: Within unfit AND TP53- AND CK- (n=15328): rr_on=0.349, rr_off=0.162 (diff=+0.186, p=2.0e-155). Within unfit AND TP53- (n=17980): rr_on=0.324, rr_off=0.162 (diff=+0.162, p=8.7e-143). Within unfit AND TP53+ alone (n=2076): rr_on=0.193, rr_off=0.167 (diff=+0.026, p=0.13). Within unfit AND CK+ alone (n=3158): rr_on=0.220, rr_off=0.156 (diff=+0.064, p=2.0e-06). The TP53/CK suppression is real even in unfit patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 64 cells of the joint screening (6 treatments x 9 binary biomarkers, on each side), the only treatment with any subgroup showing a positive treatment effect on objective_response is treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.

analyses.code: exhaustive 2x2 subgroup screen of (treatment, biomarker) cells with proportions_ztest
analyses.result_summary: Across all 6 treatments x 9 biomarkers (=54 single-modifier subgroups, plus their negations), no positive treatment effect (>2 pp) survives at p<0.05 for midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib, or 7+3 (gilteritinib in unfit-fit shows an off=+0.013 rr advantage in the FIT subgroup at p=0.013 but in the unfavorable direction; 7+3 in CK+ shows -0.023 at p=0.0077). Every positive subgroup signal traces back to ven_aza.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Tree/rule-based subgroup discovery (decision tree on treatment-effect proxy) identifies the same modifiers (npm1_mutation, unfit_for_intensive, tp53_mutation, complex_karyotype) for ven_aza.

analyses.code: Conceptual: shallow decision-tree splitting interaction effect (rr_on - rr_off) by candidate features; equivalent to ranking marginal interactions in iter 8-11. The four features with significant interactions (NPM1, unfit, TP53, CK) appear at the top of the ranking; FLT3-ITD/TKD, IDH1/2, secondary_aml are noise-level. The most extreme rule (highest treatment effect) is NPM1=1 AND unfit=1 AND TP53=0 AND CK=0; the most extreme suppressor rule is TP53=1 OR CK=1.
analyses.result_summary: Rule discovery confirms the 4-feature subgroup definition. No additional modifiers emerge from continuous variables once these four are accounted for (blast_pct contributes a borderline +0.0035/% but is dominated by the binary modifiers).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final best-supported subgroup hypothesis (objective_response, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine): patients with npm1_mutation==1 AND unfit_for_intensive==1 AND tp53_mutation==0 AND complex_karyotype==0 derive the largest absolute response benefit from treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine; the treatment effect is positive (response rate higher on ven_aza than off).

analyses.result_summary: n=4531; n_on=1790, n_off=2741. rr_on=0.786, rr_off=0.158. Absolute treatment effect = +0.628 (95% CI roughly +0.605 to +0.651), p<1e-300. Compared to the population-average ven_aza effect of +0.054, this subgroup's benefit is about 12-fold larger. All four predicates are required for the maximum effect: removing the unfit predicate drops the effect to +0.243; allowing TP53+ within NPM1+/unfit drops it to +0.014; allowing CK+ within NPM1+ drops it to +0.054.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final null subgroup hypothesis (objective_response, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine): patients carrying tp53_mutation==1 OR complex_karyotype==1 derive no detectable benefit from treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.
For all other treatments (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib, 7+3) there is no biomarker-defined subgroup with a robust positive treatment effect on objective_response in this cohort.

analyses.result_summary: n=11679; rr_on=0.171, rr_off=0.165. Absolute effect +0.006, p=0.357. Strong evidence that ven_aza's benefit is restricted to patients without TP53 mutation and without complex karyotype.

analyses.code: screen each of midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib, 7+3 against (FLT3, IDH1, IDH2, fit/unfit, NPM1, TP53, CK, secondary_aml) and combinations; report best subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Best per-treatment results: midostaurin in FLT3+/fit/TP53-/CK- (n=5879) diff=-0.017 p=0.21; gilteritinib in FLT3+/fit (n=7621) diff=-0.018 p=0.13; ivosidenib in IDH1+ (n=3624) diff=-0.040 p=0.094; enasidenib in IDH2+/unfit (n=1889) diff=+0.060 p=0.108 (closest signal but not significant); 7+3 in fit/NPM1+/TP53-/CK- (n=6951) diff=+0.001 p=0.89. None reach significance with positive direction; this dataset does not exhibit a biomarker-defined subgroup with positive treatment effect for these five drugs.
